methodsAttempted.query: site:ustc.edu.cn 中国科学技术大学 生成式人工智能 学术诚信 指导意见 学生
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found USTC official PDFs, including a finance-office research-integrity slide deck and undergraduate learning guides, but no broad student generative-AI policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ustc.edu.cn 生成式人工智能 中国科学技术大学
methodsAttempted.notes: Search found USTC news, event, research-showcase, and the 2026 NSFC application notice with explicit generative-AI application-writing rules.

methodsAttempted.query: site:teach.ustc.edu.cn 生成式人工智能 教学 指南 中国科学技术大学 教务处
methodsAttempted.notes: Search within the Teaching Office site located the 2026 undergraduate teaching-project application guide with AI-empowered education and course-construction requirements.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ustc.edu.cn 不得 生成式人工智能 论文
methodsAttempted.notes: Search confirmed the central USTC NSFC notice and an institute repost; the central www.ustc.edu.cn notice was used as the primary official source.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found two substantive USTC-controlled sources: a Teaching Office 2026 undergraduate teaching-project application guide and a central USTC 2026 NSFC application notice. Claims are scoped to undergraduate teaching-project requirements and NSFC application-writing rules. Event/training pages and official news about an external AI-era declaration were rejected as non-primary or event/news-only evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: 2026 年度本科教学工程项目申报指南
2026年度国家自然科学基金项目集中受理申请通知

stopConditions: Stop after verified USTC-controlled sources support direct AI-related claim candidates.
Do not infer a university-wide student generative-AI policy from project-application or research-funding notices.
Reject official event, training, news, and research-showcase pages when they do not contain primary policy instructions.
Do not stage raw PDFs, HTML, screenshots, browser profiles, or full normalized text.

artifacts.claimText: USTC's 2026 undergraduate teaching-project guide encourages new-course projects to integrate AI technologies with core professional competencies and to include AI application techniques, cases, and practical training in lectures and practice teaching.

artifacts.claimText: For smart-course projects under USTC's 2026 undergraduate teaching-project guide, completion standards include at least two online teaching cycles and mandatory AI learning or teaching support plus knowledge-graph functions, with two additional optional digital or AI functions.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 建设期内，课程至少基于线上课程平台开设2个教学周期，实现“必选功能2项+自选功能2项”： ①必选功能包括：AI助学或助教、知识图谱。②自选功能包括：数字教材、数字人、智能批改、智能备课、智能翻译、AI教学管理、能力图谱、素质图谱等。

artifacts.sourceTitle: 2026 年度本科教学工程项目申报指南
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 课程负责人须为本校专职教师，应主讲本实验课程2轮次以上；课程建设团队应结构合理、分工明确、素质优良，团队成员不超过5人（含课程负责人），且要有专门负责AI方面技术专家。以AI技术实现的功能包括但不限于以下内容，项目至少要实现2项功能：智能助学、学情画像、个性化任务生成、知识图谱构建、能力图谱生成。

artifacts.sourceTitle: 2026年度国家自然科学基金项目集中受理申请通知
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 申请人在撰写申请书时，如果借助生成式人工智能技术跟踪研究动态、收集整理参考文献，必须由人工核实生成式人工智能生成信息和参考文献的真实性和准确性。申请人应当对使用的生成内容负责，应当全面如实声明使用情况，按照《人工智能生成合成内容标识办法》《负责任研究行为规范指引（2023）》等国家有关规定对相关内容进行标识。

artifacts.notes: Approved as a teaching-project claim scoped to new-course applications; the source says 鼓励, so the claim uses encourages rather than mandatory wording.
Approved because the smart-course acceptance standard directly states the required teaching cycles and required AI or digital functions.
Approved with scope limited to AI plus virtual-simulation experiment teaching-course project requirements in the 2026 guide.
Approved as a research-application claim limited to USTC's 2026 NSFC notice; it does not infer a broader research AI policy.
Approved because the source directly prohibits applications generated by generative AI and unverified generated content for the 2026 NSFC application context.

artifacts.title: University of Science and Technology of China staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for University of Science and Technology of China uses two official USTC-controlled sources. The Teaching Office 2026 undergraduate teaching-project guide supports claims about AI integration in new courses, smart-course AI functions, and AI plus virtual-simulation experiment teaching-course requirements. The central USTC 2026 NSFC application notice supports claims about human verification, truthful declaration, labeling, and prohibitions on directly AI-generated applications or unverified generated content in that application context. Event/training and news-only official pages were rejected as non-primary evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are scoped to the USTC Teaching Office 2026 undergraduate teaching-project application guide and the USTC 2026 NSFC application notice.
The staged bundle does not assert a general university-wide student generative-AI policy.
The official teacher-training activity page and the official news page about an external AI-era declaration were not used as claim evidence.
No raw PDFs, HTML, screenshots, browser profiles, or logs are staged.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
